2604.28087.title: Towards Neuro-symbolic Causal Rule Synthesis, Verification, and Evaluation Grounded in Legal and Safety Principles
2604.28087.paper_summary: Rule-based systems remain central in safety-critical domains but often struggle with scalability, brittleness, and goal misspecification. These limitations can lead to reward hacking and failures in formal verification, as AI systems tend to optimize for narrow objectives. In previous research, we developed a neuro-symbolic causal framework that integrates first-order logic abduction trees, structural causal models, and deep reinforcement learning within a MAPE-K loop to provide explainable adaptations under distribution shifts. In this paper, we extend that framework by introducing a meta-level layer designed to mitigate goal misspecification and support scalable rule maintenance. This layer consists of a Goal/Rule Synthesizer and a Rule Verification Engine, which iteratively refine a formal rule theory from high-level natural-language goals and principles provided by human experts. The synthesis pipeline employs large language models (LLMs) to: (1) decompose goals into candidate causes, (2) consolidate semantics to remove redundancies, (3) translate them into candidate first-order rules, and (4) compose necessary and sufficient causal sets. The verification pipeline then performs (1) syntax and schema validation, (2) logical consistency analysis, and (3) safety and invariant checks before integrating verified rules into the knowledge base. We evaluated our approach with a proof-of-concept implementation in two autonomous driving scenarios. Results indicate that, given human-specified goals and principles, the pipeline can successfully derive minimal necessary and sufficient rule sets and formalize them as logical constraints. These findings suggest that the pipeline supports incremental, modular, and traceable rule synthesis grounded in established legal and safety principles.
2604.28087.paper_summary_zh: 基於規則的系統在安全關鍵領域仍然是核心，但常常在可擴展性、脆弱性和目標錯誤指定方面遇到困難。這些限制可能導致獎勵駭客行為和正式驗證失敗，因為AI系統傾向於優化狹隘的目標。在先前的研究中，我們開發了一個神經符號因果框架，該框架整合了第一階邏輯推理樹、結構因果模型和深度強化學習，並在MAPE-K循環中提供可解釋的適應，以應對分佈變化。在本文中，我們通過引入一個元層來擴展該框架，旨在減輕目標錯誤指定並支持可擴展的規則維護。這一層由目標/規則合成器和規則驗證引擎組成，這些組件迭代地從人類專家提供的高級自然語言目標和原則中提煉出正式的規則理論。合成管道利用大型語言模型（LLMs）來：(1) 將目標分解為候選原因，(2) 整合語義以消除冗餘，(3) 將其轉化為候選的第一階規則，並 (4) 組合必要且充分的因果集合。然後，驗證管道執行 (1) 語法和結構驗證，(2) 邏輯一致性分析，以及 (3) 安全性和不變性檢查，然後將驗證過的規則整合到知識庫中。我們在兩個自動駕駛場景中通過概念驗證實現評估了我們的方法。結果表明，給定人類指定的目標和原則，該管道可以成功推導出最小必要且充分的規則集，並將其形式化為邏輯約束。這些發現表明，該管道支持基於既定法律和安全原則的增量、模塊化和可追蹤的規則合成。
2604.28087.author: Zainab Rehan et.al.
2604.28087.authors: Zainab Rehan, Christian Medeiros Adriano, Sona Ghahremani, Holger Giese
